title: Module 4: Explainability & Audit Trail Evaluation Schema
description: Evaluates AI system explainability, decision traceability, audit trail completeness, and transparency compliance for AI-based Accounting Information Systems

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates explainability mechanisms (SHAP, LIME, etc.) enabling auditors to understand AI decisions, and meaningful transparency per GDPR Articles 13-14
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review SHAP values, LIME explanations, feature importance analyses, model documentation; verify explanations are meaningful and in plain language for data subjects
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Explanations enable decision traceability to source data, explanations produced for ≥95% of decisions, explanations meaningful and comprehensible (GDPR requirement), feature importance documented

gdpr_transparency.plain_language_requirement: Explanations understandable by average person, not just technical experts

required_fields.coverage: Percentage of decisions with explanations available

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Explainability methodology approved by AI Governance Lead and DPO/legal counsel for GDPR compliance
gdpr_articles: Articles 13-14 (Right to meaningful information about automated decision-making)

thresholds.1.0: SHAP or LIME available for ≥99% decisions, feature importance documented, explanations accurate and validated
thresholds.0.75: Explainability available for ≥95% decisions, feature importance documented
thresholds.0.5: Explainability available for ≥80% decisions or accuracy not validated
thresholds.0.0: Explainability available for <80% decisions or explanations unreliable

thresholds.1.0: Auditors can trace any decision to source data, traceability verified through testing
thresholds.0.75: Traceability exists for most decisions, minor gaps
thresholds.0.5: Traceability incomplete or verification gaps
thresholds.0.0: Cannot reliably trace decisions to source data

thresholds.1.0: Plain language explanations available for data subjects, logic/significance/consequences disclosed, legal review confirms GDPR compliance
thresholds.0.75: Plain language explanations exist, minor GDPR compliance gaps
thresholds.0.5: Explanations too technical or incomplete GDPR disclosure
thresholds.0.0: No plain language explanations or GDPR transparency requirements not met

gdpr_meaningful_information.applicability: Full weight if processing EU resident data; scored N/A if not applicable

thresholds.1.0: Explanations validated for accuracy, user testing shows comprehensibility, documentation comprehensive
thresholds.0.75: Explanations validated, documentation adequate
thresholds.0.5: Basic validation only
thresholds.0.0: No validation of explanation quality

geographic_exemption.description: GDPR transparency component scored N/A if not processing EU resident data
geographic_exemption.exemption_documentation: Geographic scope documentation showing no EU data processing

evaluation_criteria.description: Assesses completeness and integrity of AI system audit trail capturing all decisions, model versions, data lineage, and changes
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review audit logs for completeness, retention, immutability, and PCAOB AS 1105 sufficiency
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Audit trail captures 100% of AI decisions, logs retained for 7 years per SOX, logs immutable/tamper-evident, data lineage documented

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Audit trail architecture approved by IT Audit Lead, retention policy complies with SOX 404 7-year requirement
evidence_required.pcaob_standards: AS 1105 (Audit Evidence) - audit trail must provide sufficient and appropriate evidence

thresholds.1.0: 100% of AI decisions logged with all required fields
thresholds.0.75: ≥99% decisions logged, all required fields present
thresholds.0.5: ≥95% decisions logged or some fields missing
thresholds.0.0: <95% decisions logged or significant field gaps

thresholds.1.0: 7-year retention policy documented and operational, tested through sampling
thresholds.0.75: 7-year retention policy documented, testing incomplete
thresholds.0.5: Retention policy exists but <7 years or untested
thresholds.0.0: No retention policy or retention <7 years

thresholds.1.0: Tamper-evident mechanism implemented (blockchain, WORM storage, cryptographic hashing), verified through testing
thresholds.0.75: Tamper-evident mechanism implemented, not recently tested
thresholds.0.5: Partial immutability controls
thresholds.0.0: No immutability controls, logs can be modified

thresholds.1.0: Complete data lineage from source to AI decision, lineage diagram maintained, transformations documented
thresholds.0.75: Data lineage mostly documented, minor gaps
thresholds.0.5: Partial data lineage
thresholds.0.0: No data lineage documentation

evaluation_criteria.description: Evaluates model documentation quality (model cards, architecture documentation) and version control rigor
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review model cards for completeness per ISO 42001 standards, verify version control system captures all model changes
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Model cards complete with all 5 required sections, version control operational capturing all changes, model lineage traceable

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Model cards reviewed and approved by AI Governance Lead, version control tested for completeness

thresholds.1.0: Model cards complete with all 9 sections, updated within last 6 months
thresholds.0.75: Model cards complete with 7-8 sections, updated within 12 months
thresholds.0.5: Model cards exist but incomplete (5-6 sections) or outdated
thresholds.0.0: No model cards or <5 sections documented

thresholds.1.0: All model changes versioned, approvals documented, rollback capability tested
thresholds.0.75: Most changes versioned, approval process operational
thresholds.0.5: Basic version control, gaps in approval tracking
thresholds.0.0: No version control or significant gaps

thresholds.1.0: Comprehensive architecture documentation including hyperparameters, training procedure, deployment config
thresholds.0.75: Architecture documented, minor gaps
thresholds.0.5: Basic documentation
thresholds.0.0: Architecture not documented

thresholds.1.0: Documentation centrally stored, version-controlled, accessible to auditors and relevant stakeholders
thresholds.0.75: Documentation accessible, some organization gaps
thresholds.0.5: Documentation scattered or access limitations
thresholds.0.0: Documentation not readily accessible

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates availability of human intervention and review for automated decisions per GDPR Article 22 right to human intervention
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review human review procedures, request logs, reviewer qualifications, decision contestation process
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Human review available upon request, reviewers trained and qualified, review completed within 5 business days, data subjects can express point of view and contest decisions

review_procedure.request_mechanism: How data subjects request human review
review_procedure.reviewer_qualifications: Training and expertise requirements for human reviewers
review_procedure.review_timeline: Target response time (GDPR: without undue delay)
review_procedure.review_authority: Can human reviewer override AI decision?

required_fields.express_point_of_view: Mechanism for data subject to provide context/objections during review

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Human review procedure approved by DPO/legal counsel, tested within 12 months
gdpr_articles: Article 22(3) - Right to obtain human intervention, express point of view, contest decision
evidence_required.applicability: Required for solely automated decisions with legal or similarly significant effects on EU residents

thresholds.1.0: Comprehensive human review procedure documented, approved, tested within 12 months
thresholds.0.75: Procedure documented and approved, not recently tested
thresholds.0.5: Basic procedure exists, gaps in documentation
thresholds.0.0: No human review procedure

thresholds.1.0: Reviewers trained on AI system, decision domain, and GDPR requirements; competency validated
thresholds.0.75: Reviewers trained, validation incomplete
thresholds.0.5: Minimal training
thresholds.0.0: No reviewer training or competency verification

thresholds.1.0: ≥95% of reviews completed within 48 hours, 100% within 5 business days
thresholds.0.75: ≥90% within 5 business days
thresholds.0.5: ≥75% within 5 business days or average >5 days
thresholds.0.0: Frequent delays >10 business days

thresholds.1.0: Data subjects can express point of view, contest decisions, escalate; process documented and operational
thresholds.0.75: Contestation mechanism exists, minor procedural gaps
thresholds.0.5: Limited contestation process
thresholds.0.0: No contestation mechanism

geographic_exemption.description: Criterion scored N/A if AI does not make solely automated decisions with legal/significant effects OR not processing EU resident data

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates CCPA consumer rights implementation for AI-driven decisions: right to know about AI use, right to opt-out of automated decision-making, right to access logic
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review privacy policy disclosures, opt-out mechanisms, consumer request handling procedures, logic disclosure practices
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Privacy policy discloses AI use in decision-making, opt-out mechanism operational, consumers can access information about decision logic, request handling within statutory timelines

privacy_policy_disclosure.ai_use_disclosure: Privacy policy discloses use of automated decision-making technology
privacy_policy_disclosure.categories_of_data: Categories of personal information used in AI decisions disclosed
privacy_policy_disclosure.opt_out_instructions: Clear instructions for opting out of automated decision-making

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Privacy policy approved by legal counsel, opt-out mechanism tested within 12 months
evidence_required.applicability: Required if processing personal information of California residents

thresholds.1.0: Comprehensive AI disclosure in privacy policy, plain language, opt-out instructions prominent
thresholds.0.75: Disclosure exists, minor clarity gaps
thresholds.0.5: Minimal disclosure or difficult to locate
thresholds.0.0: No AI use disclosure in privacy policy

thresholds.1.0: Opt-out mechanism operational, easily accessible, processing within 15 days, alternative service available
thresholds.0.75: Mechanism operational, minor accessibility or timing gaps
thresholds.0.5: Mechanism exists but delays or accessibility issues
thresholds.0.0: No functional opt-out mechanism

thresholds.1.0: ≥95% of requests responded to within 45 days, complete information provided
thresholds.0.75: ≥90% within 45 days, mostly complete information
thresholds.0.5: ≥75% within 45 days or incomplete responses
thresholds.0.0: Frequent delays >45 days or inadequate responses

thresholds.1.0: Consumers can access meaningful information about decision logic per CPRA §1798.185(a)(16), plain language explanations provided
thresholds.0.75: Logic access available, some clarity gaps
thresholds.0.5: Limited logic disclosure
thresholds.0.0: No mechanism for consumers to access decision logic

geographic_exemption.description: Criterion scored N/A if not processing California resident personal information
geographic_exemption.exemption_documentation: Legal opinion confirming no California resident data processing

aggregate_scoring.calculation: sum(criterion_score × weight), excluding N/A criteria and renormalizing weights
aggregate_scoring.note: Privacy criteria (E4.4, E4.5) scored N/A if not applicable; weights renormalized. High threshold (0.80) reflects critical nature of explainability for audit validity.

pcaob_standards.as_1105: E4.1, E4.2 - Audit Evidence (sufficiency and appropriateness)
pcaob_standards.as_2301: E4.2 - Auditor's responses to assessed risks

nist_ai_rmf.explainable_interpretable: E4.1 - All criteria align with Explainable & Interpretable characteristic
nist_ai_rmf.measure_3_2: E4.2 - Transparency in AI system development

iso_42001.clause_9_1: E4.2 - Monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation

eu_ai_act.article_13: E4.1 - Transparency and provision of information to users

gdpr.articles_13_14: E4.1, E4.4 - Right to meaningful information about automated decision-making logic, significance, consequences
gdpr.article_22: E4.4 - Right to obtain human intervention, express point of view, contest automated decisions

ccpa.section_1798_100: E4.5 - Right to know categories and specific pieces of personal information used
ccpa.section_1798_185: E4.5 - Right to opt-out of automated decision-making
ccpa.cpra_section_1798_185_a_16: E4.5 - Right to access logic behind automated decisions and request correction

interdependency_triggers.trigger_condition: E4.1 or E4.2 < 0.7 (inadequate explainability or audit trail)
interdependency_triggers.action: Flag all other module findings as 'Lower Confidence' and escalate to human auditors as potential PCAOB AS 1105 scope limitation
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Lack of explainability prevents full validation of other modules' findings. Cannot verify governance controls, fairness metrics, security measures, or accuracy claims without adequate audit trail and explanations. Potential scope limitation in external audit.

council_of_experts_integration.stage_1_domain_score: Aggregate score from E4.1-E4.5 using weighted average

stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m1_governance: Is governance documentation itself explainable and traceable? Do RACI charts map to auditable decisions?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m2_fairness: Are fairness metrics explained to affected parties? Can auditors trace a biased decision to its root cause?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m3_security: Are security incident responses explainable and documented to PCAOB AS 1105 evidentiary standards? Are explanation artifacts secured?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m5_accuracy: Is performance degradation explainable? Can auditors verify why a model's accuracy has changed?

council_of_experts_integration.stage_3_arbitrator_inputs: M4 domain score + 4 peer review cards + explainability trigger log (critical trigger T4.1)
